name: axum
what_it_does: Ergonomic async web framework for Rust built on Tokio and Hyper. Uses a type-safe extractor system for request parsing (JSON, path params, headers, query strings) and Tower middleware for cross-cutting concerns. The official web framework from the Tokio project. Designed for building production REST APIs and agent backends with minimal boilerplate.
use_cases: Build production Rust REST API backends for agent services with type-safe request/response handling
Create high-performance HTTP servers for agent tool endpoints with zero-cost async I/O on Tokio
Implement WebSocket endpoints for real-time agent communication using axum's WebSocket extractor
Add middleware layers (auth, logging, rate limiting, CORS) using Tower middleware ecosystem
Build streaming response endpoints for LLM token streaming with axum's StreamBody support
not_for: Full-stack web applications requiring server-side templating — Actix-web has more ecosystem plugins; axum is API-focused
Teams new to Rust — axum's extractor system requires understanding Rust traits and lifetimes
Simple scripts or CLIs — axum's async setup is overkill for non-server use cases
best_when: You're building a production Rust HTTP API or agent backend and want the most ergonomic, type-safe framework with Tokio's full ecosystem.
avoid_when: You need full server-side rendering, extensive plugin ecosystem, or are new to Rust — Actix-web has a larger existing ecosystem.

auth.notes: Web framework — auth is implemented via Tower middleware layers (e.g., axum-auth, tower-http).

pricing.notes: MIT license. Part of the Tokio project.

performance.rate_limits: No rate limits built-in. Tower middleware (tower-governor) can add rate limiting. Raw throughput in millions of req/sec on modern hardware.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Extractor rejection errors return structured HTTP error responses. Custom error types implement IntoResponse for consistent API error shapes. Compile-time type errors from mismatched extractors are verbose but precise.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Framework is request-response — idempotency is application responsibility. State sharing via Arc<State> is thread-safe.
known_agent_gotchas: axum 0.6→0.7 was a major breaking change (State extractor, Router API changes) — check versions carefully when following tutorials or copying examples
Extractor order in handler function signatures matters — extractors that consume the body (Json<T>, Bytes) must appear last; multiple body extractors fail to compile
Tower middleware integration requires matching Service/Body types — type mismatches produce very long, confusing compiler error messages; use axum::middleware::from_fn for simpler middleware
Shared state must implement Clone and be wrapped in Arc — non-Clone state causes compile errors that are hard to trace
WebSocket upgrades require specific response handling — upgrade errors must be handled explicitly or the connection leaks
axum has no built-in OpenAPI/Swagger generation — use aide or utoipa crates for OpenAPI spec generation from axum routes

security_score_components.security_notes: Rust memory safety eliminates buffer overflows and use-after-free. TLS via rustls or native-tls. Type-safe request parsing prevents injection. No ambient secrets — security via application design.
